description_long: This object is a decorative horn-shaped trumpet with a body resembling polished ivory or bone, adorned with ornate metallic fittings at both ends and supported by a tripod stand. The bell end is wide and flared, featuring intricate engravings on the metallic rim, while the mouthpiece end is capped with a similarly decorated metal piece. The main body displays a natural, streaked texture, giving it an antique and handcrafted appearance. The metallic tripod stand is attached near the mouthpiece end, providing stability and elevating the horn at an angle. The overall design suggests ceremonial or display use, rather than a modern brass instrument. The color palette consists of muted ivory, gold, and subtle grayish streaks.
description: A decorative, horn-shaped trumpet with ornate gold metal fittings and a tripod stand, featuring a flared bell and streaked ivory-like body.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full curvature of the horn, the flared bell, and the gold tripod stand supporting the trumpet.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the wide, engraved bell opening and the symmetrical tripod legs extending outward.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the horn's curve, the gold fittings, and the tripod stand from a different angle.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the mouthpiece end, the gold cap, and the tripod legs extending backward.
attribute_bell_flare: The bell is wide and flared with a gradual outward curve, ending in a broad, ornate rim.
attribute_valve_configuration: No valves are present; the trumpet is a natural horn without any visible valve mechanism.
attribute_leadpipe_curvature: The leadpipe is smoothly and gently curved, following the natural arc of the horn.
attribute_tuning_slide_location: No visible tuning slide; the instrument appears to be a single, continuous piece without a separate tuning mechanism.
